edges.description: Defective collagen formation impairs tissue repair and wound healing processes.
Impaired collagen in gingival tissues leads to mucosal fragility and bleeding.
Defective collagen in joints and periarticular tissues causes pain and hemarthrosis.
Capillary fragility around hair follicles results in characteristic perifollicular bleeding.
Vascular fragility leads to easy bruising and larger areas of subcutaneous bleeding.
Hemorrhage and hemosiderin deposition cause skin pigmentation changes.

nodes.description: Joint pain resulting from hemarthrosis and periarticular hemorrhages due to capillary fragility.

evidence.snippet: Common manifestations include gingival bleeding, arthralgias, skin discoloration, impaired wound healing, perifollicular hemorrhage, and ecchymoses.

evidence.reference_title: Scurvy in a young man: a rare case report.
evidence.snippet: Here, we report on a 25-year-old male patient without any underlying conditions who presented with severe pain and ecchymoses of both lower extremities.

nodes.description: Vitamin C (ascorbic acid) is essential for hydroxylation of proline and lysine residues in collagen synthesis by prolyl and lysyl hydroxylases. Deficiency leads to inadequate hydroxyproline and hydroxylysine formation, preventing triple-helix stability and mature cross-links, resulting in defective collagen formation that affects connective tissues throughout the body.

evidence.snippet: It leads to a varied presentation, affecting multiple organ systems due to its role in the biochemical reactions of connective tissue synthesis.

nodes.description: Larger areas of subcutaneous bleeding appearing as bruises, resulting from capillary fragility and impaired collagen formation.

evidence.snippet: Common manifestations include gingival bleeding, arthralgias, skin discoloration, impaired wound healing, perifollicular hemorrhage, and ecchymoses.

evidence.reference_title: Scurvy in a young man: a rare case report.
evidence.snippet: He was diagnosed with scurvy due to a long history of staying indoors and inadequate intake of fruits or vegetables.

nodes.description: Spontaneous bleeding and swelling of the gums due to fragile mucosa and impaired collagen.

evidence.snippet: Common manifestations include gingival bleeding, arthralgias, skin discoloration, impaired wound healing, perifollicular hemorrhage, and ecchymoses.

nodes.description: Delayed or abnormal wound healing due to defective collagen synthesis and impaired tissue repair.

evidence.snippet: Common manifestations include gingival bleeding, arthralgias, skin discoloration, impaired wound healing, perifollicular hemorrhage, and ecchymoses.

nodes.description: Small hemorrhages around hair follicles due to fragile blood vessels from defective collagen.

nodes.description: Abnormal skin pigmentation and color changes associated with hemorrhage and poor wound healing.

evidence.snippet: Common manifestations include gingival bleeding, arthralgias, skin discoloration, impaired wound healing, perifollicular hemorrhage, and ecchymoses.

nodes.description: Defective collagen in blood vessel walls and basement membranes leads to capillary fragility and increased vascular permeability. This manifests as spontaneous bleeding including petechiae, ecchymoses, and mucosal hemorrhages despite normal coagulation.

evidence.snippet: Common manifestations include gingival bleeding, arthralgias, skin discoloration, impaired wound healing, perifollicular hemorrhage, and ecchymoses.
